discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_001_self_local_method_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: self write must fail when the same class already defines a zero-arg method

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_002_self_inherited_method_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: self write must fail when an inherited zero-arg method already exists

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_003_ordinary_receiver_method_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: ordinary receiver write must fail when receiver class defines inherited zero-arg method

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_004_alias_of_self_is_not_explicit_self
discovered_test_cases.description: alias of self should behave like ordinary receiver and still collide with local zero-arg method

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_005_super_write_parent_method_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: super write must fail when parent chain already defines the zero-arg method

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_006_string_subclass_builtin_like_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: write must fail when subclass defines zero-arg method length and write tries to create length attribute

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_boundary_001_parent_write_then_later_super_miss_is_51
discovered_test_cases.description: parent write behind child method is valid; later unrelated super miss should still be 51

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_boundary_002_unknown_keyword_on_object_creates_attr
discovered_test_cases.description: unknown one-argument keyword on Object should create an attribute, not raise 51

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_boundary_003_unknown_keyword_on_user_object_creates_attr
discovered_test_cases.description: unknown one-argument keyword on user object should create an attribute, not raise 51

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_boundary_004_unknown_two_arg_selector_is_51
discovered_test_cases.description: unknown multi-keyword selector cannot be treated as an attribute write and must raise 51

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_001_self_new_attr_no_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: self write is valid when no colliding zero-arg method exists

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_002_parent_creates_hidden_attr_spec_like
discovered_test_cases.description: spec-like parent method may create attribute hidden behind child method and super can read it later

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_003_child_receiver_parent_hidden_attr
discovered_test_cases.description: parent method may create attribute on child receiver and child may later read it via super

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_004_existing_slot_update_behind_child_method
discovered_test_cases.description: updating an already existing hidden attribute must stay valid

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_005_super_write_ignores_child_method
discovered_test_cases.description: super write should ignore child method and only consider parent chain for collision

discovered_test_cases.name: rt54_valid_006_copy_then_fresh_attr_no_false_collision
discovered_test_cases.description: copied Object receiver remains Object and fresh attribute write stays valid even if Main defines same zero-arg method

rt54_001_self_local_method_collision.parser_stdout:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<program language="SOL26">
  <class name="Main" parent="Object">
    <method selector="run">
      <block arity="0">
        <assign order="1">
          <var name="_"/>
          <expr>
            <send selector="foo:">
              <expr>
                <var name="self"/>
              </expr>
              <arg order="1">
                <expr>
                  <literal class="Integer" value="1"/>
                </expr>
              </arg>
            </send>
          </expr>
        </assign>
      </block>
    </method>
    <method selector="foo">
      <block arity="0">
        <assign order="1">
          <var name="r"/>
          <expr>
            <literal class="String" value="method"/>
          </expr>
        </assign>
      </block>
    </method>
  </class>
</program>
